articles.title: India Lowers Export Duty on Petrol, Diesel and ATF From June 1; Domestic Duties Unchanged
articles.description: The Centre has reduced the windfall tax on exports of petrol, diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) for the fortnight starting June 1, according to a Finance Ministry notification.

articles.title: Govt cuts windfall tax on petrol, diesel, ATF exports
articles.description: NEW DELHI, May 31:  The Government has halved windfall gains tax on export of petrol to Rs 1.5 litre, while reducing the levy on diesel to Rs 13.5/litre and aviation turbine fuel to Rs 9.5/litre effective June 1. The finance ministry in a notification said road and infrastructure cess will be nil on export of petrol and diesel. Also, there is no change in the existing duty rates on petrol and diesel cleared for domestic consumption. The special additional excise […]

articles.title: US-Iran talks, oil prices, RBI’s interest rate decision key drivers for markets this week
articles.description: Developments around the US–Iran situation, crude oil prices, and the RBI's interest rate decision would determine the trend in the equity market in an event-heavy week ahead, analysts said.

articles.title: Sri Lanka Raises Fuel Prices Amid Ongoing West Asia Tensions
articles.description: In response to the West Asia conflict, Sri Lanka's Ceylon Petroleum Corporation has once again raised fuel prices. This fifth increase sees petrol prices rise to LKR 434 per litre, with diesel and kerosene prices also adjusted upward.

articles.title: Revised export duty on fuel kicks in from June 1; Domestic excise rates on petrol, diesel remain unchanged
articles.description: The revised export levy structure includes a SAED of Rs 1.5 per litre on petrol, Rs 13.5 per litre on diesel and Rs 9.5 per litre on ATF.

articles.title: Govt revises fuel export levies for fortnight beginning June 1; no impact on domestic petrol, diesel rates - Check
articles.description: Earlier, on March 27, 2026, the export levies were introduced to ensure domestic availability of petroleum products by discouraging exports amid the ongoing tensions in West Asia. According to the notification, the revised rates have been prescribed based on the average international prices of crude oil, petrol, diesel and ATF prevailing during the period since the last review.

articles.title: Despite looming inflationary pressures, why RBI could defer rate hikes this week
articles.description: The RBI’s MPC is likely to adopt a cautious approach in its meeting due to challenges posed by rising oil prices and external risks. Growth and inflation projections may be reassessed, but interest rates are unlikely to be increased unless there is clear evidence of inflation. The West Asia conflict may lead to lower growth […]
